_comment: mcpconform (https://github.com/cejor6/mcpconform) lints this server's MCP tool surface in CI. Two info-tier rules are turned off because both flag behavior that is known and intentional here, and would otherwise be dozens of lines of unactionable noise: (1) tool/meta-namespacing -- FastMCP injects a non-reverse-DNS '_meta.fastmcp' key on every tool, which this project doesn't control; (2) provider/schema-unenforced-keyword -- tool params carry minimum/maximum as deliberate LLM-steering (see AGENTS.md 'How to add a new tool'), and OpenAI not enforcing them is expected and backstopped by authoritative runtime guards. Every other rule keeps its default severity, and any error-tier finding fails the build.
